human_verification.notes: Reviewed appliance-service boundaries, source-priority handling, dispatch and fee evidence requirements, safety and fraud routing, repeat-failure escalation, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required retail-consumer-and-hospitality source classes and domain scenario families are represented in the research summary.
All 11 required abilities are mapped to passing scenarios with no uncovered ability gaps.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging; jurisdiction and OEM-private rule variance is captured as non-blocking retrieval dependency.

specialist_owned_evidence: The pack explicitly constrains the lane to appliance-service workflow progression, bounded communication, and safe escalation rather than pricing, refund, fraud, or safety authority.
Authoritative sources and refresh policy are documented and tied to service workflow stages, evidence artifacts, and failure modes.
Scenario set includes policy exceptions, missing details, communication handoff, repeat failure, booking or fee mismatch, unsupported workaround, safety or fraud escalation, and tenant adaptation.
Materialization notes define what commons-crew must retrieve at runtime before using the package safely.
notes: The lane is safe for specification-based runtime and should not be promoted to trusted built behavior without explicit runtime approval.

Promotion to a retained implementation status requires sustained low-incident usage, validated tenant policy wiring, standardized dispatch and service adapters, and explicit implementation-owner signoff.
